dimensions.rationale: All twenty-six requirements are explicit, required, uniquely projected into the current plan, and free of placeholders, but REQ-RESOURCE promises hard semantic-call and context ceilings that its named machine authority does not completely define.

Included, excluded, and deferred scope is explicit and consistent across the specification and all reviewed source references: one neutral Python lifecycle kit, exactly five thin skills, four offline EXPERIMENTAL adapters, synthetic conformance, and no remote publication or origin-specific content.

All twenty-six acceptance ids and thirty-six evidence ids are unique, reciprocal, and projected exactly once, but AC-RESOURCE and EV-RESOURCE cannot prove the promised hard semantic-call and cumulative-context controls because the authoritative profile contains no such per-run thresholds.

Neutrality, external trust, adapter maturity, matrix authority, reviewer independence, severity, and secret-handling risks are fail-closed, but resource exhaustion through an unbounded number of logical semantic calls or unbounded cumulative context bytes per run is not governed by the profile named as immutable authority.

The source references define deterministic neutrality, routing, adapter, benchmark, platform, task-verdict, and final-audit evidence, but the resource gate lacks numeric per-run semantic-call and cumulative-context limits against which a validator can issue a fail-closed verdict.

Specification revision 2, its reciprocal plan projections, and the reviewed source references agree on the repaired neutrality, task/final severity, offline adapter, benchmark, and platform-matrix contracts; however, REQ-RESOURCE and 02-architecture-contracts.md require semantic-call and cumulative-context ceilings that agent-lifecycle-budget-profile.v1.json does not contain.

findings.message: REQ-RESOURCE requires tier-wide hard ceilings for semantic calls and context bytes, and 02-architecture-contracts.md explicitly includes cumulative context and semantic calls among resource ceilings. The named immutable machine authority defines a logicalSemanticCall measurement but no hard logical-semantic-call count per task or run; it defines contextBytesPerCall and aggregate p95 release SLOs but no hard cumulative context-bytes-per-run ceiling. semanticIterationsPerAttempt is not defined as a one-to-one semantic-call bound, and a cohort p95 is not an individual-run hard ceiling, so the promised fail-closed controls are not fully specified or testable.
findings.requiredAction: Revise the specification and its authoritative resource profile together: add explicit numeric hard logical-semantic-call and cumulative-context-byte ceilings at the required tier/task/run scopes, or narrow REQ-RESOURCE and the architecture contract to the exact enforceable limits. Define the relationship between semantic iterations and logical semantic calls, update AC-RESOURCE and EV-RESOURCE reciprocally, increment the specification revision, and obtain a new full independent review linked to this r03 artifact by exact content identity.

findings.message: The detached Ed25519 neutrality authority, trust-root ownership, signer and subject binding, freshness, zero-result conditions, temporary conformance keys, runtime gates, release scope, and reciprocal evidence remain preserved in revision 2 and its source references.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for SPEC-001; preserve these external-authority, redaction, freshness, subject-binding, runtime-gate, and release-scope semantics during the next specification revision.

findings.message: Revision 2 preserves the complete provider-neutral stage and role routing contract, deterministic capability-preserving fallback, independent review bindings, fail-closed unavailability, and exact usage attribution established after the earlier review.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for SPEC-002; retain REQ-ROUTING, AC-ROUTING, EV-ROUTING, and their exact task projection during the next specification revision.

findings.message: Revision 2 strengthens and preserves the shared offline adapter baseline, frozen normalized contract inventory, closed compatibility ranges, evidence-accurate EXPERIMENTAL inclusion, and separately authorized live VERIFIED promotion route.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for SPEC-003; keep the shared offline baseline authoritative and prevent surface-specific requirements or maturity claims from weakening it.

summary: CHANGES_REQUIRED after a full independent r03 review. Structural validation binds specification revision 2 to 255757b2f176de2ab33e09697698357f2c060f5cef5be4988815860eb7eb5b89; predecessor authority spec-review-r02 is bound by SHA-256 a06523847e3e1b10602402e32829248e81941f3bff1c6d486a740f8baaa7931d; all twenty-six required requirements have complete unique acceptance and evidence links; and prior findings SPEC-001 through SPEC-003 remain resolved. One MEDIUM blocker remains: SPEC-004, because the immutable budget profile does not define the hard semantic-call and cumulative-context-per-run ceilings promised by REQ-RESOURCE and its architecture source.
